question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released and shows CPI increased only 0.3% monthly, well below the 0.9% threshold. The official BLS report confirms the market should resolve NO, but the current market price of 32% suggests significant mispricing given the definitive data is already available.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI rose 0.3% monthly
Cleveland Fed nowcasting projected 3.16% annual rate (translating to ~0.26% monthly)
Energy supply shocks from Middle East conflict impacted but insufficient to reach 0.9%
Core CPI rose 0.4% monthly, still well below threshold
risks: Data revision possibility (though historically rare for such large changes)
Market may be pricing seasonal adjustment differences
Potential confusion between monthly vs annual inflation rates
